heading: Delete a Backup Snapshot
body:
> **Note:** If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy), you
> can't delete a backup snapshot.You can delete a snapshot using the Atlas CLI, the Atlas Administration API, or the
Atlas UI. To delete all copies of a snapshot affected by a
multi-region [snapshot copy distribution](snapshot-distribution)
policy without deleting the policy itself, delete the snapshot from its
original region.**Atlascli:**Atlas CLITo delete the backup snapshot you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas backups snapshots delete <snapshotId> [options]

```To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas backups snapshots delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-backups-snapshots-delete).**Api:**Atlas Administration API* For non-sharded clusters, delete the snapshot programmatically
  with [Remove One Replica Set Cloud Backup](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2).

* For sharded clusters, delete the snapshot programmatically with
  [Remove One Sharded Cluster Cloud Backup](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2).**Ui:**Atlas UITo delete a snapshot from the Atlas UI, perform the following procedure:
